title: ollama/ollama
summary: Official Ollama repository - get up and running with Llama 3.3, Mistral, Gemma 2, and other large language models locally.

title: ollama-python
summary: Official Python client for Ollama, available on PyPI as 'ollama'.

title: ollama-js
summary: Official JavaScript/TypeScript client for Ollama, published on npm as 'ollama'.

title: langchain-community/llms/ollama
summary: LangChain integration for Ollama models, enabling chains, agents, and RAG pipelines.

title: LlamaIndex Ollama Integration
summary: Native LlamaIndex support for local Ollama models in RAG and indexing workflows.

title: ollama-webui
summary: Feature-rich web UI for Ollama (renamed to Open WebUI), supporting multi-user, RAG, and plugins.

title: ollama-cli
summary: Interactive CLI wrapper around Ollama with chat history, syntax highlighting, and model management.

title: ollama-copilot
summary: VS Code extension that turns Ollama models into a GitHub Copilot-like pair programmer.

title: ollama-helm
summary: Helm chart to deploy Ollama on Kubernetes with GPU support and horizontal scaling.

title: ollama-docker
summary: Official Docker images for Ollama with CUDA and ROCm variants.

title: ollama4j
summary: Java/Kotlin client library for Ollama, available on Maven Central.

title: ollama-rs
summary: Rust crate providing async/sync APIs for Ollama with serde models.

title: ollama-csharp
summary: .NET client for Ollama with Blazor demo and dependency-injection support.

title: ollama-rb
summary: Official Ruby gem for Ollama with Rails integration examples.

title: ollama-go
summary: Official Go client library for Ollama with streaming and embeddings support.

title: ollama-haystack
summary: Haystack 2.0 integration for Ollama generators and embedders.

title: ollama-sdks
summary: Community-maintained list of unofficial Ollama SDKs across languages.

title: ollama-discord-bot
summary: Self-hosted Discord bot that streams Ollama model responses in chat.

title: ollama-obsidian
summary: Obsidian plugin to run local Ollama models for note Q&A and summarization.

title: ollama-gui
summary: Formerly ollama-webui, now Open WebUI - extensible self-hosted AI interface supporting Ollama.
